016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.svl;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
024
025import java.io.*;
026import java.lang.reflect.*;
027import java.util.*;
028import java.util.function.*;
029
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
033
034/**
035 * A var resolver session that combines a {@link VarResolver} with one or more session objects.
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * Instances of this class are considered light-weight and fast to construct, use, and discard.
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * This class contains the workhorse code for var resolution.
042 *
043 * <p>
044 * Instances of this class are created through the {@link VarResolver#createSession()} and
045 * {@link VarResolver#createSession(WritableBeanStore)} methods.
046 *
047 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
048 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not guaranteed to be thread safe.
049 * </ul>
050 *
051 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
052 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MarshallSimpleVariableLanguage">Simple Variable Language Basics</a>
053 * </ul>
054 */
055@SuppressWarnings({
056   "resource",   // VarResolver resources managed by calling code
057   "java:S115"   // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_contextBeanStore)
058})
059public class VarResolverSession {
060
061   // Property name constants
062   private static final String PROP_contextBeanStore = "context.beanStore";
063   private static final String PROP_sessionBeanStore = "session.beanStore";
064   private static final String PROP_var = "var";
065
066   private static boolean containsVars(Collection<?> c) {
067      var f = Flag.create();
068      c.forEach(x -> {
069         if (x instanceof CharSequence && x.toString().contains("$"))
070            f.set();
071      });
072      return f.isSet();
073   }
074
075   private static boolean containsVars(Map<?,?> m) {
076      var f = Flag.create();
077      m.forEach((k, v) -> {
078         if (v instanceof CharSequence && v.toString().contains("$"))
079            f.set();
080      });
081      return f.isSet();
082   }
083
084   private static boolean containsVars(Object array) {
085      for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(array); i++) {
086         var o = Array.get(array, i);
087         if (o instanceof CharSequence && o.toString().contains("$"))
088            return true;
089      }
090      return false;
091   }
092
093   private final VarResolver context;
094
095   private final WritableBeanStore beanStore;
096
097   /**
098    * Constructor.
099    *
100    * @param context
101    *    The {@link VarResolver} context object that contains the {@link Var Vars} and context objects associated with
102    *    that resolver.
103    * @param beanStore The bean store to use for resolving beans needed by vars.
104    *
105    */
106   public VarResolverSession(VarResolver context, WritableBeanStore beanStore) {
107      this.context = context;
108      this.beanStore = new BasicBeanStore(beanStore);
109   }
110
111   /**
112    * Adds a bean to this session.
113    *
114    * @param <T> The bean type.
115    * @param c The bean type.
116    * @param value The bean.
117    * @return This object.
118    */
119   public <T> VarResolverSession bean(Class<T> c, T value) {
120      beanStore.addBean(c, value);
121      return this;
122   }
123
124   /**
125    * Returns the bean from the registered bean store.
126    *
127    * @param <T> The value type.
128    * @param c The bean type.
129    * @return
130    *    The bean.
131    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
132    */
133   public <T> Optional<T> getBean(Class<T> c) {
134      Optional<T> t = beanStore.getBean(c);
135      if (! t.isPresent())
136         t = context.beanStore.getBean(c);
137      return t;
138   }
139
140   /**
141    * Resolve all variables in the specified string.
142    *
143    * @param s
144    *    The string to resolve variables in.
145    * @return
146    *    The new string with all variables resolved, or the same string if no variables were found.
147    *    <br>Returns <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
148    */
149   public String resolve(String s) {
150      if (s == null)
151         return null;
152      if (s.isEmpty())
153         return s;
154      return context.compile(s).resolve(this);
155   }
156
157   /**
158    * Compiles the given template string into a reusable {@link VarTemplate}.
159    *
160    * <p>
161    * Equivalent to {@link VarResolver#compile(String) this.context.compile(input)}; provided on
162    * the session for symmetry with {@link #resolve(String)}. The returned template is bound to
163    * the underlying {@link VarResolver}, not to this session — see the {@link VarTemplate}
164    * class javadoc for the compile-binding contract.
165    *
166    * @param input The template string. May be {@code null}.
167    * @return A new {@link VarTemplate} bound to the underlying resolver.
168    */
169   public VarTemplate compile(String input) {
170      return context.compile(input);
171   }
172
173   /**
174    * Returns a session-bound {@link Supplier} that re-resolves the given template against
175    * <i>this</i> session each time {@link Supplier#get()} is called.
176    *
177    * <p>
178    * Unlike {@link VarResolver#resolveSupplier(String)} — which opens a fresh session per
179    * {@code .get()} and is threadsafe to share — this Supplier captures the existing session
180    * and reuses it. <b>It is therefore not threadsafe</b>; it inherits the standard
181    * "{@link VarResolverSession} is not guaranteed to be thread safe" contract.
182    *
183    * <p>
184    * Use this variant only when the caller owns the session lifecycle and the perf saving of
185    * skipping per-call session construction matters. For shared / cross-thread use cases,
186    * prefer {@link VarResolver#resolveSupplier(String)}.
187    *
188    * @param input The template string. May be {@code null}.
189    * @return A {@code Supplier<String>} bound to this session.
190    */
191   public Supplier<String> resolveSupplier(String input) {
192      return compile(input).asSupplier(this);
193   }
194
195   /**
196    * Resolves the specified strings in the string array.
197    *
198    * @param in The string array containing variables to resolve.
199    * @return An array with resolved strings.
200    */
201   public String[] resolve(String[] in) {
202      var out = new String[in.length];
203      for (var i = 0; i < in.length; i++)
204         out[i] = resolve(in[i]);
205      return out;
206   }
207
208   /**
209    * Convenience method for resolving variables in arbitrary objects.
210    *
211    * <p>
212    * Supports resolving variables in the following object types:
213    * <ul>
214    *    <li>{@link CharSequence}
215    *    <li>Arrays containing values of type {@link CharSequence}.
216    *    <li>Collections containing values of type {@link CharSequence}.
217    *       <br>Collection class must have a no-arg constructor.
218    *    <li>Maps containing values of type {@link CharSequence}.
219    *       <br>Map class must have a no-arg constructor.
220    * </ul>
221    *
222    * @param <T> The value type.
223    * @param o The object.
224    * @return The same object if no resolution was needed, otherwise a new object or data structure if resolution was
225    * needed.
226    */
227   @SuppressWarnings({
228      "rawtypes",   // Raw types necessary for generic resolution
229      "unchecked",  // Type erasure requires unchecked operations
230      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for recursive resolution logic
231   })
232   public <T> T resolve(T o) {
233      if (o == null)
234         return null;
235      if (o instanceof CharSequence o2)
236         return (T)resolve(o2.toString());
237      if (isArray(o)) {
238         if (! containsVars(o))
239            return o;
240         var o2 = Array.newInstance(o.getClass().getComponentType(), Array.getLength(o));
241         for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(o); i++)
242            Array.set(o2, i, resolve(Array.get(o, i)));
243         return (T)o2;
244      }
245      if (o instanceof Set o2) {
246         try {
247            if (! containsVars(o2))
248               return o;
249            Set o3 = info(o).getDeclaredConstructor(x -> x.isPublic() && x.getParameterCount() == 0).map(ci -> safe(() -> (Set)ci.inner().newInstance())).orElseGet(LinkedHashSet::new);
250            Set o4 = o3;
251            o2.forEach(x -> o4.add(resolve(x)));
252            return (T)o3;
253         } catch (VarResolverException e) {
254            throw e;
255         } catch (Exception e) {
256            throw new VarResolverException(e, "Problem occurred resolving set.");
257         }
258      }
259      if (o instanceof List o2) {
260         try {
261            if (! containsVars(o2))
262               return o;
263            List o3 = info(o).getDeclaredConstructor(x -> x.isPublic() && x.getParameterCount() == 0).map(ci -> safe(() -> (List)ci.inner().newInstance())).orElseGet(() -> list());
264            List o4 = o3;
265            o2.forEach(x -> o4.add(resolve(x)));
266            return (T)o3;
267         } catch (VarResolverException e) {
268            throw e;
269         } catch (Exception e) {
270            throw new VarResolverException(e, "Problem occurred resolving collection.");
271         }
272      }
273      if (o instanceof Map o2) {
274         try {
275            if (! containsVars(o2))
276               return o;
277            Map o3 = info(o).getDeclaredConstructor(x -> x.isPublic() && x.getParameterCount() == 0).map(ci -> safe(() -> (Map)ci.inner().newInstance())).orElseGet(LinkedHashMap::new);
278            Map o4 = o3;
279            o2.forEach((k, v) -> o4.put(k, resolve(v)));
280            return (T)o3;
281         } catch (VarResolverException e) {
282            throw e;
283         } catch (Exception e) {
284            throw new VarResolverException(e, "Problem occurred resolving map.");
285         }
286      }
287      return o;
288   }
289
290   /**
291    * Resolves variables in the specified string and sends the output to the specified writer.
292    *
293    * <p>
294    * More efficient than first parsing to a string and then serializing to the writer since this method doesn't need
295    * to construct a large string.
296    *
297    * @param s The string to resolve variables in.
298    * @param out The writer to write to.
299    * @return The same writer.
300    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
301    */
302   public Writer resolveTo(String s, Writer out) throws IOException {
303      if (s == null)
304         return out;
305      if (s.isEmpty()) {
306         return out;
307      }
308      return context.compile(s).resolveTo(this, out);
309   }
310
311   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
312      // @formatter:off
313      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
314         .a(PROP_contextBeanStore, this.context.beanStore)
315         .a(PROP_var, this.context.getVarMap().keySet())
316         .a(PROP_sessionBeanStore, beanStore);
317      // @formatter:on
318   }
319
320   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
321   public String toString() {
322      return r(properties());
323   }
324
325   /**
326    * Returns the {@link Var} with the specified name.
327    *
328    * @param name The var name (e.g. <js>"S"</js>).
329    * @return The {@link Var} instance, or <jk>null</jk> if no <c>Var</c> is associated with the specified name.
330    */
331   protected Var getVar(String name) {
332      Var v = this.context.getVarMap().get(name);
333      return nn(v) && v.canResolve(this) ? v : null;
334   }
335}
